behavior_summary: Coordinates and runs a multi-stage 'fire spell' effect: spawns temporary visual/effect items, orients them toward a target point or entity, runs per-effect behavior scripts (projectiles/motes), plays SFX, applies impact/damage worker calls at legal map points, optionally recenters the camera, teleports/repositions an NPC/subject, and then cleans up temporary items.

The routine creates short-lived items that expand or move from a source toward target coordinates, repeatedly checks placement legality and distance thresholds, and spawns helper scripts (METHOD::013E and FIRESPEL subroutines) at chosen coordinates to apply the actual impact/effect.
The overall entry acts as a process: it spawns concurrent pieces (visual timers/free cleaners), may set temporary avatar stasis during key phases, and destroys input temporary items when finished.
notable_control_flow: Top-level process entry U8P_FIRESPEL::0080: spawns helper processes (visual/cleanup), destroys an input temp item, then returns.

U8P_FIRESPEL::00A9: calculates direction from a source item to given (x,y), creates and places effect items at computed offsets, optionally centers camera, calls a local initializer METHOD::0113, and spawns visual sub-scripts (U8P_FIRESPEL::1F65) plus FREE timers to manage lifecycle.

U8P_FIRESPEL::059A: a runtime behavior loop for a spawned element (projectile/mote). It resolves targets, possibly sets avatar stasis, orients movers toward targets via Item::getDirToItem/Coords, checks Item::legal_move and a distance metric (FREE::1CE3), plays sound (playSFX), spawns impact worker METHOD::013E at legal points, and repeats/respawns until completion.
Many placement/spawn decisions are guarded by legal_move checks and distance comparisons, preventing invalid placements and controlling expansion radius.
open_questions: Exact gameplay effect of METHOD::013E (spawned repeatedly at impact points) — likely applies damage/visual hit effects but not conclusively identified.
Precise semantics of METHOD::0113, METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7 and other spawned METHODs: their animation/state changes are inferred only.
When and why the camera is centered (Camera::setCenterOn) — appears optional/conditional and may depend on context (e.g., caster vs. avatar view).
Full intent and rule-set for avatar stasis: which phases set/getAvatarInStasis and whether it blocks player control only or other processing.
Teleport/map semantics: whether Npc::teleport always preserves map or can cross-map — observed Npc::getMap used but exact behavior uncertain.

main_function.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::0080
main_function.purpose: Entry/coordination process for the fire-spell effect: spawn and start visual/timed helpers, orchestrate per-instance effect creation and runtime loops, perform final teleport/reposition of affected NPC(s) or targets, and clean up temporary items.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the fire-spell is executed (spell cast or area trigger)
Called with parameters: target_item (Item*), x (uword), y (uword), z (ubyte) indicating the spell's target position/context

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::0080
function_map.role: Top-level orchestrator process for the fire spell (spawns helpers, handles cleanup and final teleportation/repositioning).

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::00A9
function_map.role: Compute direction to target coords, create and initialize temporary effect items, optionally recenter camera, and spawn visual/timer helpers to start an effect instance.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::059A
function_map.role: Runtime loop for a spawned moving/expanding effect element (projectile/mote): target processing, movement/placement validation, SFX, impact spawns, and lifecycle termination/respawn logic.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::1F65
function_map.role: Spawned visual/temporal sub-script contributing to the spell's animation lifecycle.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::2AF5
function_map.role: Internal helper invoked during runtime loop for specialized impact or movement logic (inferred).

function_map.name: METHOD::0113
function_map.role: Local method used to initialize/configure newly created effect items (orientation/animation parameters).

internal_calls.purpose: Create and initialize individual effect instances at computed offsets from source and start their lifecycle.
Spawn concurrent visual/animation sub-processes that run the spell's visuals independently.
Start the per-instance visual/temporal script after creating the effect item and running initialization.
Call internal routines for impact checks or specialized movement/effect logic during the runtime loop.

external_calls.purpose: Destroy input temporary item(s) passed into the spell, cleaning up used resources.
Compute direction from the source item to target coordinates to orient newly created effect items.
Create temporary effect items used for visuals/projectiles.
Optionally recenter the camera on the source/created effect to focus the player's view during the effect.
Spawn a short timer/cleanup helper to free resources or act as a delayed placeholder referencing a spawned pid.
Resolve or pick a target entity for homing/orientation during the mover's runtime loop.
Query and temporarily set avatar stasis state while critical portions of effect run (freeze/unfreeze player).
Orient mover/projectile toward a target item/entity.
Check if a placement/move of an effect item to a worldpoint is legal; used to avoid invalid spawns.
Compute a distance/metric between points for range checks deciding when to spawn impact effects.
Spawn worker that applies the spell's effect (damage/impact visuals/status) at chosen coordinates.
Play sound effects associated with projectile/mote movement or impact.
Query an NPC's current map id before teleporting to ensure teleport uses the correct map context.
Teleport or reposition an NPC/target to the computed destination coordinates after effect resolves.
Trigger item touch behaviour when an effect item contacts an entity or completes to cause interactions.

reads.name: inDaemonScene
reads.likely_effect: Selects variant orientation/behavior in the runtime loop (affects mover orientation/animation).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Coordinates and runs a multi-stage 'fire spell' effect: spawns temporary visual/effect items, orients them toward a target point or entity, runs per-effect behavior scripts (projectiles/motes), plays SFX, applies impact/damage worker calls at legal map points, optionally recenters the camera, teleports/repositions an NPC/subject, and then cleans up temporary items.
